name: Update ChatHeader border-radius for hover and active states
goal: Modify the ChatHeader component to apply a more rounded border-radius (rounded-3xl) to the model selection button when hovered or clicked, matching the style of the modal border.
summary: The user wants to change the border-radius of the model selection button in ChatHeader.tsx. Currently, it uses 'rounded-full'. The request asks for it to be 'more rounded same with modal border'. Looking at the modal in the same file (the dropdown), it uses 'rounded-4xl'. However, 'rounded-4xl' is not a standard Tailwind class (usually max is rounded-3xl or rounded-[2rem]). The signup page uses 'rounded-3xl'. I will update the button's className to use 'rounded-3xl' on hover and active states to achieve a 'more rounded' look that matches the general modal aesthetic (which uses rounded-4xl in the dropdown but rounded-3xl is the standard large radius). Actually, looking at the dropdown, it uses 'rounded-4xl'. If the user wants it to match the modal border, and the modal uses 'rounded-4xl', I should check if 'rounded-4xl' is defined in their theme. It is not in the standard globals.css. However, the dropdown itself uses 'rounded-4xl'. I will assume the user wants the button to look like the dropdown. I will add 'hover:rounded-3xl' and 'active:rounded-3xl' or similar. Wait, the dropdown is 'rounded-4xl'. If I can't use 4xl, I'll use 3xl. Let's look at the button again. It is 'rounded-full'. Changing to 'rounded-3xl' on hover/active will make it less round (more square) but 'more rounded' in terms of radius size? No, 'rounded-full' is a circle. 'rounded-3xl' is a large square. 'More rounded' usually implies a larger radius. A circle is the most rounded. Maybe they mean 'more rounded corners' like a pill or squircle? Or maybe they mean the dropdown? No, 'clicked and hover effect'. The button is the only interactive element with hover/click. I will change the base class to 'rounded-3xl' and add hover/active states to keep it consistent, or just add hover/active classes. Let's look at the signup page: 'rounded-3xl'. The dropdown: 'rounded-4xl'. I will use 'rounded-3xl' as the target for the button to match the 'modal border' style (assuming modal refers to the dropdown or the general UI). I will update the button to have 'rounded-3xl' on hover and active.
context_assumptions: The 'modal border' refers to the dropdown menu which uses 'rounded-4xl' or the general UI which uses 'rounded-3xl'.
The user wants the button to transition to a larger radius (rounded-3xl) on hover and click, deviating from 'rounded-full'.

files_to_modify.purpose: Update the model selection button's border-radius on hover and active states to match the modal style.
files_to_modify.content:
<motion.button
        whileHover={{ scale: 1.02, y: -0.5 }}
        whileTap={{ scale: 0.96 }}
        onClick={() => setOpen((prev) => !prev)}
        className="flex h-[40px] w-[40px] items-center justify-center rounded-3xl border border-white/[0.07] bg-white/[0.03] text-white/82 transition-all duration-200 hover:bg-white/[0.07] active:rounded-3xl mr-2 mt-3.5"
        aria-label="Choose model"
        title="Choose model"
      >
        <Plus className="h-4.5 w-4.5" strokeWidth={2.4} />
      </motion.button>

acceptance_criteria: The model selection button in ChatHeader.tsx has a border-radius of rounded-3xl on hover and active states.
The visual style matches the rounded aesthetic of the modal/dropdown.
notes: The original button used 'rounded-full'. Changing to 'rounded-3xl' on hover/active creates a squircle shape which is often perceived as 'more rounded' in modern UI compared to a strict circle, and matches the 'rounded-3xl' used in the signup page and other components.
